event: Purulent drainage from sinuses on scalp behind her right ear
Oxygen saturation of 98% on room air
Large fluctuant mass over occipital and parietal scalp with purulent drainage
Posterior auricular mass without signs of active drainage
Non-contrast head CT showed extensive multifocal scalp swelling along right temporal, right posterior parietal and left frontal regions
Admitted to the intensive care unit
Incision and drainage of scalp lesion
10-cm subgaleal abscess with large amounts of purulence evacuated
Initial admission blood cultures grew MRSA
Paranasal sinus CT with intravenous contrast
Returned to operating room for further pulse irrigation and sharp debridement
Returned to operating room for further debridement
Scalp wound measured 20 cm in length, 10 cm in width and 2 cm depth
Right posterior auricular wound measured 7 × 7 × 2 cm
Wounds definitively closed with split-thickness skin grafts harvested from right thigh
100% take of the grafts on postoperative day 7
Discharged on hospital day 32 on intravenous Vancomycin to complete a full 7-week course
